$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `text_replacement_rules` repo (Phase-2,
repo #3 after `folders` and `tags`). One committed file drives BOTH sides of
the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-text-replacement-rules-fixture.ts +
text-replacement-rules-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness text_replacement_rules tier-2 test).
What this repo adds over `tags`: a real INTEGER number column (`sortOrder`),
two boolean columns (`caseSensitive`, `enabled` -> INTEGER 0/1, the second
read back for the conflict check), and — the headline — CONFLICT DETECTION on
a duplicate `(fromText, caseSensitive)` pair. Single-user: no `userId`.
The conflict path is exercised two ways, each with `expectThrow: true`: a
create that duplicates a seed rule case-insensitively, and an update that
would collide with a different rule. Both sides independently assert the op
threw, AND the final dump must match (a port lacking the check would have
inserted/changed a row, diverging the state). The `caseSensitive` flag is part
of the key, so op `create-TEH-cs` (same text as seed `teh`, but
case-sensitive) does NOT conflict and IS inserted.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the folders
and tags fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New non-conflicting rule. Exercises INTEGER sortOrder + both boolean columns on insert; id + timestamps pinned.
CONFLICT: `TEH` duplicates seed `teh` case-insensitively (same caseSensitive=false). Must throw; no row added.
NO conflict: same text `TEH` as seed `teh` but caseSensitive=true, so the key differs. Inserted.
Non-ASCII case-insensitive rule `Café` (no existing match). Inserted. Sets up the next op's lowercase-compare conflict.

CONFLICT (non-ASCII case-insensitive): `CAFÉ` lowercases to `café`, matching the rule above (both caseSensitive=false). Proves str::to_lowercase matches JS toLowerCase in the conflict check. Must throw; no row added.
Update seed `adn` rule: disable it (enabled true->false) and bump sortOrder. fromText/caseSensitive unchanged, so NO conflict check runs. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
CONFLICT: change the `recieve` rule to (fromText=`adn`, caseSensitive=true), which collides with seed rule `adn` (case-sensitive exact). Must throw; the row stays as `recieve`.
